duplicate_commercial_positions: No duplicate commercial-position IDs discovered in parsed governed rows; narrative overlap preserved for review.
duplicate_offers: Offer duplicates are preserved by source document/row in canonical offer intelligence; no automatic merge performed.
duplicate_supplier_references: Supplier name repeats are preserved by source document/row; no automatic merge performed.
duplicate_opportunities: Opportunity narrative overlap detected between market-participant whitespace propositions and offer hypotheses; no automatic reconciliation performed.
inconsistent_terminology: Commercial Position package expected by commission maps to banking-reinvention-hypotheses-release.zip / reinvention hypothesis terminology in uploaded estate.
docs/research requested path differs by case from actual docs/Research path.
Provider offer, supplier footprint, offer fit, and reinvention offer hypothesis are distinct terms and are not collapsed.

BK-CON-001
Branches are a cost burden versus branches are trust/member-value infrastructure.
Lloyds/incumbent branch/access pressure; industry distribution pressure.
Nationwide branch/member-value evidence.
Participant type, ownership and value allocation differ.
High
Prevents generic branch strategy interpretation; same access change has different implications.
Participant-specific branch economics and customer outcome evidence.
BK-CON-002
High digital engagement proves primacy versus high digital engagement does not prove whole-base primacy.
Challenger active usage and digital engagement evidence.
Observation that primacy metrics lack salary-flow/direct-debit/all-customer proof.
Metric definitions and denominator differ.
High
Prevents overclaiming challenger displacement of incumbents.
Customer-flow and cohort-level account usage evidence.
BK-CON-003
Cloud enables resilience and speed versus cloud creates systemic concentration and exit risk.
Cloud/core migration and challenger low-legacy evidence.
Critical third-party and infrastructure dependency evidence.
Both are true at different control layers.
High
Requires opportunity interpretation to include controls, exit and dependency, not just migration acceleration.
Workload maps, exit plans and resilience tests.
BK-CON-004
AI productivity claims versus AI assurance/control burden.
Bank AI and colleague/customer augmentation evidence.
Observation that independent benefits are weak and review/assurance burden increases.
Claims often come from enterprise/supplier statements; control burden is regulatory/operational.
Medium/High
Prevents treating AI as a simple productivity opportunity.
Benefit baselines, outcome evidence, model governance evidence.
BK-CON-005
Santander global platform acceleration versus local UK ring-fenced accountability.
Santander group platform/ONE/Gravity/AI/TSB ambition evidence.
Santander UK local regulation, customer, mortgage/deposit and conduct accountability.
Dual-control model; not a true contradiction but a tension requiring governance separation.
High
Commercial route may sit at UK bank, group platform or integration boundary.
Decision-right and integration governance evidence.
BK-CON-006
Barclays can be represented by one Enterprise Twin versus Barclays needs additional candidate mechanisms.
Barclays universal group Twin can represent the whole enterprise.
Investment Bank dynamics require mechanisms beyond retail/commercial BM set.
Architecture can hold the enterprise, but mechanism catalogue may need extension.
High
Prevents using a retail-banking lens for universal-bank opportunity interpretation.
Mechanism acceptance and capital-markets deepening.
BK-CON-007
Bank-owned customer experience versus infrastructure-owned reachability and settlement.
Bank UX and fraud/account-state control evidence.
Payments Infrastructure Twin control-boundary evidence.
Customer sees bank, but many payment behaviours are rail/scheme/settlement dependent.
High
Prevents misattributing all payment opportunity or failure to the bank participant.
Participant-specific payment-incident and infrastructure transition evidence.
Contradiction ID
Proposition in tension
Commercial consequence
Supporting / conflicting evidence
Treatment
BK-OSI-CON-001
Supplier partnership versus accessible whitespace
A participant can show strong offer fit while a major incumbent supplier already publicly occupies the same domain.
Offer fit may be real but commercially inaccessible without a complementary seam.
NatWest AWS/Accenture; Lloyds Google Cloud; Nationwide Accenture/Form3/Broadcom; Barclays Microsoft.
Do not convert fit into pursuit; preserve as Shape/Validate evidence need.
BK-OSI-CON-002
Customer-facing AI momentum versus trust/control risk
Starling and Barclays show visible AI adoption, while Banking mechanisms preserve conduct, trust, operational resilience and fraud-control constraints.
Supports Secure AI Governance but weakens any generic autonomous-AI positioning.
WEB-012; WEB-013; BK-SRC-MECH; BK-SRC-OBS.
Offer must be governed and outcome-evidenced.
BK-OSI-CON-003
Cloud modernisation as opportunity versus cloud concentration as risk
Cloud is an enabler for AI/data, but CTP designation turns cloud dependency into regulated resilience risk.
Hybrid Cloud and Cloud Modernisation fit must include resilience and exit optionality.
WEB-005; BK-SRC-INFRA; WEB-001; WEB-003; WEB-008.
Treat cloud offers as regulated operating model, not pure migration.
BK-OSI-CON-004
Digital challenger fit versus in-house platform strength
Monzo and Starling fit data/AI themes, but their commercial position includes strong in-house digital/platform capability.
Generic transformation offers may be less suitable even when the mechanism is relevant.
BK-SRC-CP; WEB-009; WEB-011; WEB-012.
Use industry variant or specialist assurance/additive roles.
BK-OSI-CON-005
Mutual access differentiation versus efficiency pressure
Nationwide’s member/access posture supports Customer Operations Transformation but may resist branch-reduction framing.
Offer positioning must avoid incumbent cost-takeout assumptions.
BK-SRC-CP; BK-SRC-MPI; WEB-006; WEB-008.
Frame as member-value/service sustainability where evidence supports.
BK-OSI-CON-006
Supplier-authored evidence versus independent delivery proof
Several supplier-footprint sources are supplier-authored and may overstate benefits.
Confidence in relationship can be high while confidence in realised outcome remains medium or low.
Separate relationship visibility from proof of benefit.

broken_references: Not all inherited IDs resolve inside the three uploaded packages; many intentionally reference pre-existing governed Banking assets.
